question: How do I register for courses at ICU?
gold_answer: At the start of each term you must register online for courses (at least one). Even if you are only registering for Pre-Registration or a Senior Thesis, you must register online, meet your advisor for an interview, obtain the advisor’s online approval, and confirm the registration on Registration Day. Complete the Course Registration Procedure by the deadline—changes outside the specified period are not accepted; if you cannot register on Registration Day, use the registration change period. Registration confirmation is the student’s responsibility. [1][2]

retrieved_passages.title: General Education Courses｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
retrieved_passages.body_text: General Education Courses ICU General Education Courses are not simply introductory courses. They represent the opportunity to learn about various academic fields and gain exposure to the quintessence of Liberal-Arts. Therefore, General Education Courses are made available throughout all four years of undergraduate study, depending on individual interests or learning progress. By taking these courses alongside Specialized Courses, it is possible to view one's major field of study from a different perspective, and understand it in relation to other fields of study. Key Points of General Education Students can take General Education Courses throughout all four years.

retrieved_passages.body_text: FAQ Admissions Graduate School in General Preparation for Admission to Graduate School I am interested in applying for the ICU Graduate School, but can I specialize in an area unrelated to my undergraduate study? It is possible to apply to study a different field of specialization from your undergraduate major. However, since all Master's students need to complete 30 credits' worth of related courses and write a master's thesis within two years, they are expected to have already acquired at least basic knowledge in the desired field of specialization. We also recommend that applicants submit a Research Plan in as much detail as possible, to increase their chances of being shortlisted when the application documents are screened.

question: What are the rules for course withdrawal?
gold_answer: Registration Withdrawal means withdrawing ALL course registration for the ongoing term (partial withdrawal is not allowed). Permission may be granted by the CLA Dean / GS Dean for illness or other unavoidable reasons recognized by the University. Apply to the Educational Affairs Group with supporting documents (e.g., medical certificate); the application must be submitted by one month prior to the last day of classes. If approved, no grades are given and all courses are recorded as “W” (not included in GPA); the term still counts toward university residence requirements for graduation [1][2].

retrieved_passages.title: ehandbook - Registration Withdrawal
retrieved_passages.body_text: 10. Auditing Courses » Schedule for Course Registration Registration Withdrawal Registration Withdrawal is to withdraw ALL course registration for ongoing term. It is not possible to withdraw the course registration partly. If a student cannot continue his/her study for ongoing term due to the illness or unavoidable reason recognized by the University, permission for Registration Withdrawal may be given by the CLA Dean / GS Dean. Application for Registration Withdrawal shold be submitted to Educational Affairs Group together with the documents explaining the reasonable reasons, such as a medical certificate. Application must be submitted by a month prior to the last day of the classes.

question: How does ICU calculate GPA?
gold_answer: ICU assigns grade points A=4, B=3, C=2, D=1, E=0. GPA is calculated by multiplying each course’s grade point by its credit units, summing those products, and dividing by the total attempted credits. Repeated-course credits are included; only courses graded A–E are counted in the calculation [1][3].

retrieved_passages.title: GPA｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
retrieved_passages.body_text: GPA The GPA system: An objective way for students to look at their academic performance "GPA" (Grade Point Average) corresponds to the average grade a student has earned to date per course credit. As a standard for measuring academic performance, a student's GPA can have an impact on everything from his or her eligibility for financial aid to study abroad screening procedures. By expressing grades in number form, GPA gives students an objective way to look at their academic performance levels and provides faculty members with a valuable resource for advising students more effectively on what courses to take. Calculating GPA A: Superior (4 pt.) B: Excellent (3 pt.) C: Good (2 pt.) D: Passing (1 pt.) E: Failing (0 pt.)

retrieved_passages.title: ehandbook - 成績平均点数（GPA）
retrieved_passages.body_text: 成績平均点数（GPA） 本学では学業成績をはかる基準として、履修した科目の点数合計を単位数で除する成績平均点数(Grade Point Average)を採用しています。 成績点数は，Ａ＝4点，Ｂ＝3点，Ｃ＝2点，Ｄ＝1点，Ｅ＝0点として，該当する点数に科目単位数を乗じて算出されます。 成績平均点数（GPA）は成績点数の合計を総履修単位数で除すことで算出されます。その際，再履修した科目の単位も集計に加えられます。Ａ〜Ｅ以外の科目の単位は集計に加えられません。 成績平均点数 = (科目単位数 × 成績点数の和) ／ 総履修単位数

question: What housing options are available for ICU students?
gold_answer: ICU offers on‑campus educational dormitories (multiple houses such as Momi, Maple, Global, Zelkova, Ginkgo, Oak, Third/Fourth Women’s House, Canada House) and undergraduate/postgraduate residence buildings [2][5]. If you do not secure a dorm room, ICU Service Co., Ltd. assists students in finding off‑campus housing; homestay options are also available [1][4].

question: How can I apply for student dormitories?
gold_answer: Apply via the ICU Housing Office — watch the Housing Office webpage for application announcements (e.g., information for September 2026 was scheduled to be posted around mid‑May). For questions or details contact housing-office@icu.ac.jp or visit the Housing Office (Dialogue House 1F) during office hours [1].

question: What should I do if I need counseling support at ICU?
gold_answer: Contact the ICU Counseling Center for psychological support—staff include clinical psychologists, counselors, and a psychiatrist—and use their individual counseling, group counseling, or workshops to address issues like adjustment, relationships, career, and mental/physical health [1]. For other topic-specific or alternative consultation options (face-to-face, phone, chat/SNS), check the Health Care Office mental health consultation list. Note most listed contact points may not offer English support [2][3].

retrieved_passages.body_text: Counseling, Human Rights Consultation and Health Services Counseling Center ICU Counseling Center provides psychological support to help ICU students find better solutions to difficulties and challenges encountered during the university life. Some common issues students discuss in counseling include adjustment to university life, interpersonal relationships, self-understanding, career paths after graduation, and mental and physical health. The staff members at the counseling center are clinical psychologists, counselors, and a psychiatrist with experience in university counseling. Individual counseling, group counseling, and workshops are among the various services offered by the counseling center.

question: Where can I find the ICU campus map?
gold_answer: The ICU campus map is available on the “Facilities and Campus Map” page of ICU’s website: https://www.icu.ac.jp/en/about/campus [2].

retrieved_passages.title: Facilities and Campus Map｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
retrieved_passages.body_text: Facilities and Campus Map ICU is located on a campus of roughly 620,000 square meters. Our classroom buildings and research facilities are surrounded by the Musashino woods, dotted with student dormitories and faculty housing. The western side of the campus adjoins the Tokyo Metropolitan Nogawa Park. With its rich natural surroundings, ICU is a great place to enjoy the changing seasons. The Ministry of the Environment (MOE) certified our campus as a Nationally Certified Sustainably Managed Natural Site under the name of "ICU Mitaka Campus Woodland" in October 2023.

question: What is the role of foundation courses in the ICU curriculum?
gold_answer: Foundation courses provide students with core academic skills and an academic grounding: undergraduates take foundational courses from many majors to develop core skills and clarify their interests before later specialization, while graduate Program Foundation Courses give disciplinary grounding (e.g., in social sciences) and teach research design and methodology [2][4].

retrieved_passages.title: ehandbook - Curriculum Structure
retrieved_passages.body_text: Curriculum Structure 1. About the Curriculum ｜ 2. Curricular Changes ｜ 3. Academic Integrity About the Curriculum Since ICU's College of Liberal Arts has only one division, Division of Arts and Sciences, there is no need for students to choose a field of specialization prior to entering the university. This means students are able to fully pursue their individual academic interests. Immediately after entering ICU, students start taking General Education Courses and courses in either the English or Japanese language program, and through the completion of these courses they will be able to acquire a strong academic foundation and skills in critical thinking.

retrieved_passages.title: Your Four Years at ICU｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
retrieved_passages.body_text: Your Four Years at ICU A curriculum that does not dictate specialization at the time of matriculation Once enrolled at ICU, students begin taking courses in Languages (English/Japanese), General Education, Physical Education, and other College-Wide Courses. Students also take foundational courses from a selection of over 30 majors (areas of specialization) in the humanities and sciences, developing core academic skills as they identify their individual interests and aptitudes. This approach comes from ICU's belief in "later specialization," which gives students more time to study a variety of subjects before deciding on a major.

retrieved_passages.body_text: Repeating/Retaking Rule ｜ 10. Auditing Courses » Schedule for Course Registration Repeating/Retaking Rule There are two categories for taking the same course again; REPEAT: in order to improve the grade of a course that has been passed. RETAKE: in order to pass a course that a student has failed. A student may repeat a course once in order to improve his/her grade at any time that the same course is offered. A student may retake a course till he/she passes the course at any time that the same course is offered. The results of the attempt shall be recorded in his/her cumulative record: The grade, the unit, the course number and the course title.

question: What is the academic integrity policy at ICU?
gold_answer: ICU requires all faculty, staff, and students to learn and understand its academic integrity policy; students should consult a professor if unsure and work is evaluated on the assumption of that understanding. Students may not submit work that violates the policy (e.g., reusing work for another course, submitting another’s work, or commercially purchased papers) and may not claim ignorance; penalties vary by severity and repeat offenses [1][3][5]. ICU emphasizes critical thinking and states that text produced by generative AI (even if altered) is not considered a student’s own work and is a violation when tasks must be completed individually [4].

Students may not submit work that contains violations of the academic integrity policy and then seek leniency claiming that they were ignorant of the policy. Punishment for violations of the academic integrity policy vary depending upon the severity of the offence and whether the offence is a first or repeat offence.

retrieved_passages.body_text: Japanese Language Program (JLP) The Right Course for Each and Every One of You Master Japanese Language and Broaden your World From its founding days, Japanese Language Program (JLP) have been providing Japanese language courses to ICU students as part of the College of Liberal Arts. There are around 300 students from about 40 different countries enrolled in JLP courses every year, including degree-seeking regular four-year students, one-year exchange students, and graduate students. It is an opportunity for both international students and Japanese students to become proficient in Japanese. JLP aims to facilitate students to gain functional communication competence in Japanese.

retrieved_passages.title: Japanese Language Program (JLP)｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
retrieved_passages.body_text: I am living in Japan, I truly feel glad that I challenged myself. How were the Japanese Language Program (JLP) classes? On attending the classes, I was surprised to learn that the number of students assigned to each faculty member was extremely small and that the classes promoted interactive learning. I participated in the Japanese Program where Japanese is taught as a foreign language. The J5 course consisted of 4 faculty members and 20 students, and the students were able to seek advice freely if they had any questions. Furthermore, all lectures, discussions, and handouts were in Japanese, and I feel that the immersive education in Japanese helped me to steadily improve my Japanese language ability.

gold_answer: Start with ICU’s Graduate School Overview page for program structure, areas of specialization, and general features of the graduate school [1]. For practical guidance on choosing a program and preparing your research plan, see the Graduate School FAQ (check faculty profiles, contact faculty, and review curriculum/syllabi and degree requirements) [5]. gold_answer: The retrieved evidence is insufficient to answer this question.

retrieved_passages.title: ehandbook - Transferring Credits from Other Universities
retrieved_passages.body_text: Transferring Credits from Other Universities Students who fit the following criteria and also receive approval from the Dean of the College of Liberal Arts are eligible to transfer credits for coursework completed at universities other than ICU. Effective from AY2013, coursework completed at other universities while on leave of absence from ICU may be approved for credit transfer in accordance with the credit transfer for Accredited Privately Financed Ryugaku (Study Abroad ) (However, transferred credits will be counted only as Electives). The courses approved are for credit transfer only. Grades are not transferred and those do not affect GPA at ICU.

retrieved_passages.title: Information on Transfer of Credits (for Transfer Student)｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
retrieved_passages.body_text: Information on Transfer of Credits For Transfer Student: We would like to provide information about credit transfer to give you some idea which of the courses you took at your previous institution may or may not be granted credits at ICU. 1. Courses Eligible for Transfer of Credits Students who have completed courses at other universities before entering ICU may transfer those credits if the courses are considered equivalent to courses offered at ICU, and are approved as being applicable towards ICU graduation requirements, and if the grades received were 70 points or higher out of 100. *NOTE: Credit transfers will be granted only after students complete the required procedures after entering ICU.

The grades for the courses must be D (60 points) or higher. question: Where can I find information about PE or health and physical education requirements?
gold_answer: Find requirements on ICU’s Physical Education / Health and Physical Education webpage, which outlines the graduation requirement (Health Science + PE Exercise I–III) and program details [1]. For course registration rules and notes (e.g., one PE EX per term, pre-registration, adapted PE), see the eHandbook Pre-Registration page for Physical Education exercise courses [3].

retrieved_passages.title: Physical Education｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
retrieved_passages.body_text: Physical Education As a graduation requirement, ICU students must take one course of Health Science (worth one credit ), which comprises lectures on health and physical education, as well as the three courses Physical Education Exercise I, II, and III (together worth one credit, i.e. one-third of a credit per course), which consist of practical exercises. In these courses, students learn not only about health and safety in student life but also gain knowledge and skills for improving quality of life over their entire lifetime. In addition, through the exercise courses students learn firsthand about improving communication skills in group activities, leadership, and followership, actively and independent-mindedly, with fun.

retrieved_passages.title: Physical Education｜ICU - INTERNATIONAL CHRISTIAN UNIVERSITY
